en.q:
An object is placed in front of a convex lens. The image formed has the same size as the object. The
separation between the object and the image is 40 cm. Which of the following statements is/are
correct?
(1) The focal length of the convex lens is 20 cm
(2) If the object is move slightly towards the lens, the distance between the object and the image will
be larger
(3) If the object is move slightly away from the lens, the image will become smaller
en.exp: (1) is incorrect, the image has the same size as the object, the object is located at 2𝐹. Therefore, the focal length should be 10 𝑐𝑚. (2) is correct, 𝑓= 𝑢+ 𝑣 , when 10 = 18 + 𝑣 , 𝑣= 22.5 𝑐𝑚. When 𝑢 change to 18, the distance between the image and the object become 40.5 𝑐𝑚. (3) is correct, 𝑓= 𝑢+ 𝑣, when 𝑢 increase, 𝑣 decrease, the image will be smaller.
en.hint: (1) is incorrect, the image has the same size as the object, the object is located at 2𝐹.

en.q:
An object is placed in front of a lens and a plane mirror as shown. The image is formed at where the
object is. What is the focal length of the lens?
en.exp: When parallel light ray enters the lens, the light ray will be converged to the focus. The parallel light ray reflected from the mirror are converged to a point 30 𝑐𝑚 from the lens, thus the focal length of the lens is 30 𝑐𝑚.
en.hint: When parallel light ray enters the lens, the light ray will be converged to the focus.

en.q:
Which of the following statements about the following experiment is/are correct?
(1) The focal length of the convex lens is equal to the distance
between the convex lens and the lamp-housing.
(2) The brightness and shape of the image will not change if
the plane mirror is moved a little bit backwards.
(3) The shape of the image will not change if the upper half
of the lens is covered by a card.
en.exp: Only when the object is located at the principal focus of the convex lens, rays from the object are parallel to the principal axis after passing through the lens. These parallel rays are reflected by the plane mirror and then converge to the principal focus of the lens after passing through the lens once again. ∴ (1) is correct. The reflection of the parallel rays by the plane mirror is unaffected if the plane mirror is moved a little bit backwards. ∴ (2) is correct. When the convex lens and the plane mirror are placed at positions so that a clear image is formed on the screen, the size of the image equals to that of the object. ∴ (3) is incorrect.
en.hint: Only when the object is located at the principal focus of the convex lens, rays from the…

The distance between the boy and the image is 2.5 + 2 = 4.5 𝑚 (1) is incorrect, the lower edge of the mirror will not be able to reflect the light from the feet to her eyes. (2) and (3) are correct. en.q:
Which of the following statements concerning total internal reflection of light is/are correct?
(1) The angle of reflection equals to the critical angle.
(2) Total internal reflection of light will not occur when light enters an optically less dense medium.
(3) The critical angle depends on the refractive index.
en.exp: C and D are incorrect. The angle of incidence from A to P should be the same as the angle of refraction from P to A. Since they come from the same formula of 𝑛𝑃sin 𝜃𝑃= 𝑛𝐴sin 𝜃𝐴. A is incorrect since light travel slower in A than in P, the light ray should bend away from normal when it enters P from A. (1) is incorrect, the angle of refraction should be same as the angle of incidence. (2) is incorrect, total internal reflection will only occur when light ray enters a less dense medium from a denser medium. (3) is correct, sin 𝐶= 𝑛.
en.hint: C and D are incorrect.

en.q:
Which of the following statement(s) about the virtual image is/are correct?
(1) Virtual image must be erect.
(2) Virtual image cannot be captured by a camera.
(3) Virtual image can be seen by the naked eyes.
en.exp: (1): Correct. Min length of mirror = half of the height = 0.5(1.8) = 0.9 m (2): Incorrect. The image is the same size as object, regardless of object distance (3): Incorrect. He can still see his whole image, regardless of object distance For the portion above the hole, vertical distance that he can see = 2(10) = 20 cm. Therefore, the maximum interval above the hole that he can see is 5 cm For the portion below the hole, vertical distance that he can see = 2(5) = 10 cm. Therefore, maximum interval above the hole that he can see is 35 cm Required portion = from 5 cm to 35 cm (1): Correct. Virtual image formed by mirror and lens are erect (2): Incorrect. Only real image can be captured by a camera (3): Correct. We can observe the image formed by mirror through our naked eyes
en.hint: (1): Correct.

en.q:
In an insulated container, 50 g of steam at 100 C is injected uniformly on a glass plate. What is the final temperature
of the glass plate? The mass of glass plate is 1.5 kg and its initial temperature is 25 C.
Given:   Specific latent heat of vaporization of water = 2.26  106 J kg−1
        Specific heat capacity of water = 4200 J kg−1 C−1
Specific heat capacity of glass = 840 J kg−1 C−1
en.exp: Energy released to change 100 ℃ Steam to 100 ℃ water: ΔE1 = m1 lv = 0.05 × ( 2.26 × 106 ) = 1.13 × 105 J Energy required to change 25 ℃ water to 100 ℃ water: ΔE2 = m2 cΔT = 1.5 × 840 × (100 − 25 ) = 94 500 J Since ΔE2 < ΔE1, the final mixture is 100 ℃, i.e. not all steam condensed.

en.q:
Metal block A is in contact with another metal block B, and they finally reach thermal equilibrium.
Block A experiences a temperature rise of 10 °C and block B experiences a temperature drop of 5 °C.
Assume there is no heat loss to the surroundings. Which of the following is correct?
choices: The initial temperature of A is higher than that of B.
The energy gained by A is larger than the energy lost by B.
The average kinetic energy of A increases during the heat exchange process.
The internal energy of B remains the same during the heat exchange process
en.exp: A: As metal block A experiences a temperature rise, it is gaining energy. It means that the initial temperature of A is lower than that of B B: By the conservation of energy, energy loss = energy gain C: The higher the temperature, the higher the average KE is. D: PE of B remains unchanged. As temperature decreases, the average KE decreases, thus internal energy decreases
en.hint: A: As metal block A experiences a temperature rise, it is gaining energy.

en.q:
Substances X and Y of the same mass are heated at the same rate. The graphs below show their temperature–
time graphs. Initially, they are in solid state. Which of the following physical quantities of substance X are
higher than that of substance Y according to the graph?
choices: The specific latent heat of fusion and the specific heat capacity in solid state
The specific latent heat of fusion and the specific heat capacity in liquid state
The melting point and the specific heat capacity in solid state
The melting point and the specific heat capacity in liquid state
en.exp: The length of horizontal line in Y is longer than that in X. As both have the same mass and are heated at the same rate, specific latent heat of Y is greater than that of X. A and B are incorrect The height of horizontal line represents melting point. Therefore, X has a higher melting point 𝑠𝑝𝑒𝑐𝑖𝑓𝑖𝑐 ℎ𝑒𝑎𝑡 𝑐𝑎𝑝𝑎𝑐𝑖𝑡𝑦 ∝ Slope at solid/liquid state Slope of solid X > Slope of solid Y 𝑐𝑋ሺ𝑠ሻ< 𝑐𝑌ሺ𝑠ሻ Slope of liquid X < Slope of liquid Y 𝑐𝑋ሺ𝑙ሻ> 𝑐𝑌ሺ𝑙ሻ D is correct (1): The y-value of the first horizontal line of X is greater than that of the first horizontal line of Y. As there is not any temperature change during fusion, it indicates that X has a higher melting point (2): The slope of Y in liquid state is greater than that of X in liquid state. By Pt = C𝛥𝑇, 𝑆𝑙𝑜𝑝𝑒= 𝛥𝑇 𝑡= 𝑃 𝐶. As P to X and Y is the same, Y should have a smaller heat capacity than X (3): As the masses of X and Y are unknown, the specific latent heat of fusion cannot be determined
en.hint: The length of horizontal line in Y is longer than that in X.

en.q:
Two substances P and Q, both in solid form initially, are heated up by two heaters of equal power. The figure

Which of the following statements about P and Q must be correct?
(1) P has a lower melting point than Q.
(2) P has a larger specific latent heat of fusion than Q.
(3) The solid form of P has a smaller heat capacity than that of Q.
en.exp: The stable temperature means the substance is changing its state. While P has a lower stable temperature than Q, so P has a lower melting point than Q. Thus, (1) is correct. Masses of P and Q aren’t given, so specific latent heat of fusion can’t be determined. Thus, (2) is incorrect ∆𝑇 𝑡= 𝑃 𝐶 , 𝑠𝑙𝑜𝑝𝑒= 𝑃 𝐶

Masses of P and Q aren’t given, so specific latent heat of fusion can’t be determined. Thus, (2) is incorrect ∆𝑇 𝑡= 𝑃 𝐶 , 𝑠𝑙𝑜𝑝𝑒= 𝑃 𝐶
zhHant.hint: The stable temperature means the substance is changing its state.
